openmeter/subscription/addon.purpose: Manages subscription addon lifecycle: domain types (SubscriptionAddon, SubscriptionAddonInstance, SubscriptionAddonQuantity), the Service interface, events (CreatedEvent, ChangeQuantityEvent), and the RateCard extend/restore logic. Primary constraint: quantities are an append-only timeline; instances are derived by pairing adjacent quantity entries into open periods.

patterns.name: Append-only quantity timeline
patterns.description: SubscriptionAddonQuantity rows are never updated or deleted. Quantities are stored as a timeutil.Timeline and GetInstances() derives SubscriptionAddonInstance values by pairing adjacent open periods.

patterns.name: Apply/Restore RateCard mutation
patterns.description: SubscriptionAddonRateCard.Apply adds price/entitlement/discount deltas to a target RateCard pointer; Restore subtracts them. Both require a non-nil pointer target and non-nil annotations map. Restore uses instanceType to decide whether to nil out a SingleInstance price.
patterns.example: rc.Apply(target, annotations) // target must be *productcatalog.FlatFeeRateCard or *UsageBasedRateCard

patterns.name: CreatedEvent / ChangeQuantityEvent via marshaler.Event
patterns.description: Domain events implement marshaler.Event (EventName() + EventMetadata() + Validate()). Event names follow the pattern io.openmeter.subscriptionaddon.v1.subscriptionaddon.<action>. Use NewCreatedEvent / NewChangeQuantityEvent constructors to capture session.UserID from ctx.

patterns.name: Input type Validate() method
patterns.description: All input structs (CreateSubscriptionAddonInput, CreateSubscriptionAddonQuantityInput) implement Validate() returning a joined errors.Join error. Call Validate() before passing to service.
patterns.example: if err := inp.InitialQuantity.Validate(); err != nil { return fmt.Errorf("initialQuantity: %w", err) }

patterns.name: GetInstances() derives instances from DeletedAt truncation
patterns.description: If SubscriptionAddon.DeletedAt is set, GetInstances() first filters quantities via timeline.Before(deletedAt) before pairing periods, ensuring soft-deleted addons surface only active instances.

key_file_guides.role: Core domain types (SubscriptionAddon, CreateSubscriptionAddonInput) and GetInstances() / GetInstanceAt() derivation logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetInstances() relies on GetOpenPeriods() having exactly len(quantities) periods after the zeroth sentinel — any change to Timeline semantics breaks instance derivation.

key_file_guides.role: SubscriptionAddonInstance: the virtual merged view of addon + quantity for a time period.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Instance is purely derived; never persist it directly.

key_file_guides.role: SubscriptionAddonQuantity type and CreateSubscriptionAddonQuantityInput with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Quantity 0 is valid for CreateSubscriptionAddonQuantityInput (used to signal removal) but CreateSubscriptionAddonInput rejects quantity==0 for initial creation.

key_file_guides.role: Apply and Restore methods on SubscriptionAddonRateCard — additive/subtractive price, entitlement, and discount mutations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Apply/Restore must receive a pointer RateCard and non-nil annotations. Restoring a flat price to negative or entitlement count to negative is an error.

key_file_guides.role: CreatedEvent and ChangeQuantityEvent implementing marshaler.Event.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EventName must stay stable — it is a Watermill routing key.

key_file_guides.role: SubscriptionAddonRepository and SubscriptionAddonQuantityRepository interfaces used by service and repo sub-packages.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SubscriptionAddonQuantityRepository.Create only — there is no Update or Delete method by design.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface: Create, Get, List, ChangeQuantity.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChangeQuantity appends a new quantity row; it does not update the previous one.

anti_patterns: Calling SubscriptionAddonRateCard.Apply or Restore with a nil target or nil annotations — both are rejected with explicit errors.
Mutating SubscriptionAddonQuantity rows after creation — they are immutable append-only records.
Setting Quantity=0 on CreateSubscriptionAddonInput.InitialQuantity — Validate() returns an error; 0-quantity is only valid for subsequent ChangeQuantity calls.
Reading annotation map keys by string literal instead of via subscription.AnnotationParser.
Deriving instance periods manually instead of using GetInstances() — the derivation logic with DeletedAt truncation is non-trivial.

decisions.rationale: Preserves full audit history of quantity changes and enables point-in-time instance derivation without mutable state.

extend.go owns the single-RateCard mutation semantics; diff/ orchestrates multi-item spec-level application. Keeping them separate prevents the pure in-memory diff layer from gaining business-logic dependencies.

decisions.decision: Events published inside transaction (in service sub-package)
decisions.rationale: Ensures DB write and event publish are consistent; rollback prevents orphaned events.

code_examples.scenario: Add a boolean-entitlement addon rate card to a target rate card

openmeter/subscription/hooks.purpose: Organisational folder grouping SubscriptionCommandHook implementations that maintain subscription invariants across lifecycle events. Currently contains hooks/annotations which repairs doubly-linked chain annotations on subscription deletion. Hooks here must never import subscription.Service (write path) to avoid re-entrant hook calls.

patterns.name: Embed NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook
patterns.description: All hook implementations embed subscription.NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook so only the overridden methods (e.g. BeforeDelete) need to be implemented. This prevents future interface changes from breaking existing hooks.

patterns.name: Use SubscriptionRepository for writes, not Service
patterns.description: Hook implementations call subscription.SubscriptionRepository.UpdateAnnotations directly rather than subscription.Service to avoid re-entrant hook invocations.

patterns.name: Soft-skip on NotFound, hard-error on others
patterns.description: When a linked subscription is not found during annotation cleanup, log and skip — it may have been deleted in a race. Any other error is returned as a hard failure.

key_file_guides.role: Implements BeforeDelete to clean up previous/superseding subscription ID annotations on chain-linked subscriptions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must clone annotations before mutating; must nil-out empty maps after key removal.

anti_patterns: Calling subscription.Service write methods from inside a hook — creates re-entrant hook invocations.
Mutating annotation maps in-place without cloning first.
Not embedding NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook — forces explicit implementation of all hook methods.
Returning an error on subscription.IsSubscriptionNotFoundError — these are expected concurrent-delete races.

decisions.decision: Hooks placed in a dedicated sub-package rather than inline in subscription/service
decisions.rationale: Prevents circular imports between the service and cross-cutting lifecycle concerns like annotation chain repair.

openmeter/subscription/testutils.purpose: Shared test fixtures and dependency builders for the subscription domain. Provides SubscriptionDependencies (a fully wired set of real services built from Ent adapters), example entities (ExampleRateCard1–5, ExampleAddonRateCard1–6, ExampleFeatureKey*), comparison helpers (ValidateSpecAndView, SpecsEqual, SubscriptionAddonsEqual), and mocks (MockService, MockWorkflowService, TestPatch). Must NOT import app/common to avoid import cycles.

patterns.name: NewService builds full dependency graph from package constructors
patterns.description: NewService(t, dbDeps) wires all real adapters and services (customer, subject, entitlement, plan, addon, subscription, workflow) from their underlying constructors, not from app/common Wire providers, to avoid import cycles.

patterns.name: SetupDBDeps provisions a real Postgres DB with full migration
patterns.description: SetupDBDeps(t) calls testutils.InitPostgresDB, runs migrate.Up(), and returns DBDeps{DBClient, EntDriver, PGDriver}. Tests must call deps.Cleanup(t) via t.Cleanup.

patterns.name: Example* vars as canonical test data
patterns.description: ExampleRateCard1–5, ExampleAddonRateCard1–6, ExampleFeatureKey/2/3, ExampleNamespace, ISOMonth are package-level vars. Always clone rate cards before using as plan/subscription input to avoid test-to-test mutation.

patterns.name: testAddonService wraps addon.Service with CreateTestAddon helper
patterns.description: CreateTestAddon creates and immediately publishes the addon, which is the required two-step flow. Use deps.AddonService.CreateTestAddon(t, inp) in tests rather than calling service.CreateAddon then PublishAddon separately.

patterns.name: MockService and MockWorkflowService for unit tests
patterns.description: MockService satisfies subscription.Service; MockWorkflowService satisfies subscriptionworkflow.Service (without Restore/AddAddon/ChangeAddonQuantity). Wire Fn fields for the methods under test; leave others nil to get panics on unexpected calls.

key_file_guides.role: NewService — the canonical wiring entry point for subscription integration tests. SubscriptionDependencies struct holds all assembled services.
key_file_guides.watch_for: After SetDBClient, read back meterID via GetMeterByIDOrSlug — pgtestdb template reuse can resolve to a different ID.

key_file_guides.role: SetupDBDeps with mutex-guarded Postgres provisioning and full migration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: sync.Mutex guards serial DB setup; parallel subtests must each call SetupDBDeps independently.

key_file_guides.role: ValidateSpecAndView deep-compares a SubscriptionSpec against a SubscriptionView including entitlement alignment, feature linking, and phase cadences.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UsagePeriod alignment checks use Truncate(time.Minute) — avoid sub-minute time precision in test fixtures.

key_file_guides.role: CreatePlanWithAddon, CreateSubscriptionFromPlan, CreateAddonForSubscription — high-level test scenario builders.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateAddonForSubscription encodes a Quantity=0 terminal entry to represent a closed cadence; use CreateMultiInstanceAddonForSubscription for multi-instance addons.

key_file_guides.role: testPlanbuilder and testSubscriptionSpecBuilder fluent DSLs for plan and spec construction in tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: testSubscriptionSpecBuilder.Build() calls SyncAnnotations and Validate — always use Build() rather than using the spec directly.

key_file_guides.role: MockService and MockWorkflowService with Fn fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MockWorkflowService does not implement Restore, AddAddon, or ChangeAddonQuantity — calling these will panic.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common from testutils — causes import cycles and violates the boundary that test deps must be built from package constructors.
Using ExampleRateCard* vars directly as plan input without Clone() — shared package-level vars will be mutated across tests.
Calling service.CreateAddon without immediately calling PublishAddon — use CreateTestAddon which does both.
Constructing SubscriptionDependencies manually instead of via NewService — misses hook registration (annotationCleanupHook, entitlementValidatorHook) which are required for correct test semantics.
Using context.Background() instead of t.Context() in test helpers.

decisions.decision: All services built from package constructors, not app/common Wire providers
decisions.rationale: Prevents import cycles; keeps test wiring independent from production DI changes.

decisions.decision: ValidateSpecAndView validates entitlement alignment against billingAnchor
decisions.rationale: Entitlement UsagePeriod must be anchored to subscription BillingAnchor — this is a billing correctness invariant that tests must enforce.

code_examples.scenario: Set up a full subscription integration test with plan and addon
code_examples.code:
import subscriptiontestutils "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/openmeter/subscription/testutils"

func TestMyFeature(t *testing.T) {
    dbDeps := subscriptiontestutils.SetupDBDeps(t)
    t.Cleanup(func() { dbDeps.Cleanup(t) })
    deps := subscriptiontestutils.NewService(t, dbDeps)

    plan, addon := subscriptiontestutils.CreatePlanWithAddon(t, deps,
        subscriptiontestutils.GetExamplePlanInput(t),
        subscriptiontestutils.GetExampleAddonInput(t, period),
    )
    subView := subscriptiontestutils.CreateSubscriptionFromPlan(t, &deps, plan, clock.Now())
    subscriptiontestutils.ValidateSpecAndView(t, expectedSpec, subView)
}

openmeter/subscription/validators.purpose: Organisational folder for cross-domain validators that enforce subscription pre-conditions on other domain mutations. Contains two sub-packages: validators/customer (blocks customer delete/update when active subscriptions exist) and validators/subscription (unique-subscription-per-customer invariant as a SubscriptionCommandHook). Both are registered via their respective service's RegisterHook/RegisterRequestValidator in app/common wiring.

patterns.name: Embed noop base types
patterns.description: Both validators embed their respective noop base (NoopRequestValidator / NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook) so only the relevant methods are overridden, future interface additions don't break compilation.
patterns.example: type customerValidator struct { customer.NoopRequestValidator; subSvc subscription.Service }

patterns.name: Register in app/common, not in domain package
patterns.description: Validators are registered via customer.Service.RegisterRequestValidator() and subscription.Service.RegisterHook() in app/common wiring, keeping circular-import boundaries clean.

patterns.name: Use models.NewGeneric* errors for HTTP mapping
patterns.description: All validator errors use models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError or models.NewGenericNotFoundError so the HTTP layer maps them to 412 or 404 correctly.

key_file_guides.role: Blocks customer delete/subject-key update when active subscriptions exist. Uses clock.Now() for ActiveAt filter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must filter by ActiveAt — without it, cancelled subscriptions are returned and block valid deletes.

key_file_guides.role: Enforces unique-subscription invariant before Create/Update. Feature-flag-gated between per-subscription and per-feature uniqueness.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must exclude the current subscription ID when checking uniqueness on Update; must use pagination.CollectAll to avoid silently missing subscriptions beyond first page.

key_file_guides.role: Pure utility functions for subscription period and feature overlap checks.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Business logic decisions belong in validator.go, not components.go.

anti_patterns: Calling subscription.Service write methods from within a SubscriptionCommandHook — creates re-entrant hook calls.
Using context.Background() instead of the caller-supplied ctx.
Returning plain fmt.Errorf instead of models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError — breaks HTTP status code mapping.
Querying subscriptions without the ActiveAt / ActiveInPeriod filter — returns all-time subscriptions and breaks uniqueness check.

decisions.decision: Validators placed in a dedicated validators/ sub-folder
decisions.rationale: Avoids circular imports: subscription depends on customer, so subscription validators of customer cannot live in the subscription package root.
MultiSubscriptionEnabledFF allows per-feature rather than per-subscription uniqueness checking for tenants that need multiple concurrent subscriptions.

openmeter/subscription/workflow.purpose: Public interface package for the subscription workflow layer. Defines the Service interface (CreateFromPlan, EditRunning, ChangeToPlan, Restore, AddAddon, ChangeAddonQuantity), input/output types, AnnotationParser for workflow-scoped annotations, and MapSubscriptionErrors for converting spec validation errors to HTTP-mappable generic errors. The concrete implementation lives in workflow/service/.

patterns.name: MapSubscriptionErrors wraps spec errors
patterns.description: All workflow operations wrap spec-level errors through MapSubscriptionErrors before returning so SpecValidationError becomes GenericValidationError (400) and AlignmentError becomes GenericConflictError (409).

patterns.name: AnnotationParser for workflow annotations
patterns.description: subscriptionworkflow.AnnotationParser.SetUniquePatchID stamps a ULID on annotations to identify an edit operation. Always use AnnotationParser instead of writing annotation keys by string literal.

patterns.name: Input types with Validate()
patterns.description: AddAddonWorkflowInput and ChangeAddonQuantityWorkflowInput implement Validate(). Call Validate() before passing to service methods.
patterns.example: if err := inp.Validate(); err != nil { return models.NewGenericValidationError(err) }

patterns.name: EditRunning accepts timing parameter
patterns.description: Service.EditRunning takes a subscription.Timing parameter (as of the updated interface) — the concrete implementation in workflow/service/ uses this to resolve the effective edit timestamp.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface and all input/output types. Single source of truth for the workflow API surface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EditRunning signature includes timing — callers that omit it will use zero-value Timing which resolves to clock.Now().

key_file_guides.role: MapSubscriptionErrors — must be called on every error returned from a workflow operation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only SpecValidationError and AlignmentError are remapped; all other errors pass through unchanged.

key_file_guides.role: AnnotationParser with SetUniquePatchID for stamping edit operations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Key constant AnnotationEditUniqueKey must stay stable — it is persisted in the DB.

anti_patterns: Returning spec errors without wrapping via MapSubscriptionErrors — SpecValidationError will not map to 400 and AlignmentError will not map to 409.
Writing annotation keys by string literal instead of using AnnotationParser.

Implementing workflow operations directly on subscription.Service instead of through the workflow layer — workflow operations require locking, transactions, and addon sync that the core service does not provide.

decisions.rationale: Workflow operations are multi-step (lock+tx+addon sync+spec update) and compose core service methods; combining them would violate single responsibility and make the core service untestable in isolation.

decisions.decision: MapSubscriptionErrors in workflow package rather than service package
decisions.rationale: The workflow layer is the HTTP-facing boundary; mapping domain errors to HTTP status codes at this boundary keeps the core service pure.

code_examples.scenario: Call EditRunning and map spec errors to HTTP status codes
